data.title: Report predicts India to be 3rd largest economy by 2030, USD 30 trillion growth by 2047. IT and digital services key drivers.
data.description: A report by MarketsandMarkets titled "Future of India - Forecast to 2035" predicts that by 2030, India will become the world's third-largest economy, surpassing Japan and Germany. The country is projected to achieve a USD 30 trillion growth trajectory by 2047 driven by demographic advantages and ongoing reforms. India is expected to surpass Japan to become the fourth-largest economy by 2026 with sustained GDP expansion. Despite global uncertainties and election cycles, India's economy shows strength and resilience. Strong macroeconomic fundamentals, demographic advantage, urbanization, and structural reforms are key factors propelling India's long-term growth trajectory, with the IT and digital services industry playing a significant role. The report details the industries contributing to India's GDP growth and positioning it as a global economic powerhouse. To download the full report, visit https://www.marketsandmarkets.com/pdfdownloadNew.asp?id=153099933.

data.title: SEKISUI CHEMICAL creates PFAS-free pipes for semiconductor manufacturing. Tighter regulations driving demand. President: Keita Kato. Urban Infrastructure & Environmental Products Company developing new technology.
data.description: In Tokyo, SEKISUI CHEMICAL CO., LTD. (TOKYO: 4204) under President Keita Kato revealed plans to address stricter global regulations on perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) by introducing PFAS-free pipe materials for ultrapure process applications. The Urban Infrastructure & Environmental Products Company, led by President Yoshiyuki Hirai, is spearheading this initiative in response to the increasing demand for environmentally friendly solutions. This innovation signifies the company's commitment to reducing the environmental impact and aligning with evolving regulatory standards worldwide.

data.title: CEO deems stock 'most misunderstood' despite $10B Q2 profit; aiming to raise $4.2B for Bitcoin play.
data.description: CEO Phong Le of Strategy stated that the market has yet to fully comprehend and appreciate its involvement in Bitcoin, despite the company generating a significant profit of $10 billion in the second quarter. Strategy intends to raise $4.2 billion in funds to further bolster its Bitcoin investment.
